notes.note: Latin ergastula: the barrack-prisons in which chained agricultural slaves were kept. Releasing and arming them was a sign of Antony's desperation after Mutina and a slur on the quality of his new troops.
Vada Sabatia (near modern Vado Ligure) on the Ligurian coast, where Ventidius brought his column over the Apennines to join Antony. Not to be confused with Vada Volaterrana in Etruria.

notes.anchor: I had four hundred thousand sesterces and more of my own
notes.note: The sesterce (HS) was the standard Roman accounting coin; 400,000 was the property qualification for the equestrian order, so Decimus is saying he has spent a full knight's census on the war.

notes.anchor: not if I had Varro's treasuries could I keep up with the expense
notes.note: M. Terentius Varro, the great scholar and reputedly very wealthy landowner, here stands proverbially for vast riches.
Latin iii Non. Mai., the third day before the Nones of May counted inclusively, i.e. 5 May. Dertona (modern Tortona) lay on the road Decimus was following in pursuit of Antony.
